lessonTitle: How Benign is Sickle Cell Trait?

items.html: Displays each point with a bullet
Is similar to a PowerPoint slide

data.html:
Before we dive into more about Sickle Cell Trait (SCT), let's take a quick look at the historical context for SCD:

  • 1910 – James B Herrick first described abnormally shaped cells under microscope
  • 1922 – Verne Mason named disease – sickle cell anemia
  • 1940s – Irving Sherman noticed birefringence but was unsure of what was causing it

Sickle cell disease (umbrella term for homozygous and heterozygous sickle conditions) is a result of a point mutation in the beta globin chain. This point mutation causes a switch from glutamic acid to valine amino acid at codon 6 of beta hemoglobin chain, on chromosome 11. This causes the switch from glutamic acid to valine amino acid. The valine-type hemoglobin causes red cells to sickle when exposed to a low oxygen threshold.

Patient with sickle cell trait inherits HbS from one parent and HbA from the other parent making them heterozygous.

slides.title: How Benign is Sickle Cell Trait?

data.html:
SCT has been positioned as a 'benign' condition involving a mutation on one beta chain of the hemoglobin molecule.

There are over 150,000 Ontarians with the sickle cell trait, which is an underestimation since there are still so many people who do not know their sickle cell status.

There is better tracking of the number of people with the trait since the inclusion of SCD in newborn screening in November of 2006.

Literature has long supported and proclaimed SCT as a benign condition; that the person is said to be a carrier; to see no manifestations of sickle cell disease, such as pain crisis and anemia, but that's not always the case.

The movement of the HbS allelle from mostly areas endemic with malaria to countries worldwide, can be visually seen in the photo below. Click on the photo and move the map around to see where the HbS allelle is found in high numbers on the top world map and the way it coincides with malaria endemic regions, shown in bottom world map.

Photo Credit: https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(10)61029-X/abstract

The HbS allele is found worldwide affecting all genders and races. This disease was originally coined as a Black persons disease, but we must dismantle this thinking and perpetuation of incorrect information. Slavery, immigration, and interracial marriages have contributed to the spread of the HbS allele, but ignorance is what continues to drive it's proliferation.

slides.title: Do you know your sickle cell status?

data.question: A blonde haired, blue eyed, Caucasian person from England can have sickle cell trait
data.feedback_correct: The HbS allele is found worldwide affecting all genders and races.

slides.title: Clinical Manifestations of Sickle Cell Trait (HbAS)

data.html:
Papillary Necrosis

What Causes Splenic Infarction?

The pathogenesis causing splenic infarction is similar to other complications. It can occur when patient's are:

  • Exposed to low oxygen environments in high altitudes (greater than 1100 meters or 3600 feet above sea level)
  • Dehydrated
  • Increased acidity in blood
  • Increased blood viscosity

Unlike the other complications, it also occurs even when the patient is at rest at low altitudes

Splenic Infarction

Renal Medullary Carcinoma

Hyphema and Glaucoma in Sickle Cell Disease

Rhabdomyolysis is the breakdown of skeletal muscle cells during physical exertion causing myoglobinuria.

Myoglobin is a cytoplasmic hemoprotein in cardiac and skeletal muscles, that transports oxygen. Myoglobinuria is the presence of myoglobin in the urine.

In the context of sickle cell trait and during physical exertion, there is a 54% higher rate of rhabdomyolysis.

An autopsy revealed Dave Lloyd II died from acute exertional rhabdomyolysis secondary to sickle cell trait after intense football training. (Photo credit: www.chron.com , 2008) .

The death of Lloyd, college freshman, led to the screening policy implemented by the NCAA.

data.text: Although sickle cell trait has been associated with many complications, the average life expectancy of people with sickle cell trait is the same as the general population.

items.html: An estimated 150, 000 Ontarians have the sickle cell trait and 300 million worldwide.
More attention needs to be paid to individuals with sickle cell trait and efforts should be made to reduce the percentage because it not always completely benign.
Individuals with sickle cell trait have been shown to develop sickling of the hemoglobin leading to organ damage when exposed to hypoxia, high altitude, dehydration, excessive exercise, etc
Ashorobi, D., Ramsey, A., Naga, S., Yarrarapu, S., & Bhatt, R. (2022). Sickle Cell Trait. StatPearls. Retrieved from https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK537130/
Rees, D., Williams, T., & Gladwin, M. (2010) Sickle-cell-disease. Retrieved from: https://www.thelancet.com/action/showPdf?pii=S0140-6736%2810%2961029-X
Ashorobi, D., Ramsey, A., Killeen, R., & Bhatt, R. (2024). Sickle Cell Trait. StatPearls retreived from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK537130/
Subramaniam, S & Chao, J. (2016). Managing Acute Complications of Sickle Cell Disease in Pediatric Patients https://www.ebmedicine.net/topics/allergic-immunologic-inflammatory/pediatric-sickle-cell

candidates.suggestion: Learning Objectives bullets are EdApp placeholder text ('Has a several points', 'Displays each point with a bullet', 'Is similar to a PowerPoint slide') — not real objectives. Confirm whether real objectives should replace these or whether the slide should be dropped.
candidates.reason: The source Markdown contains the same placeholder used in other SCAGO modules; the actual lesson objectives were never authored into this section.

candidates.suggestion: The section header 'Learn more about Sickle Cell Trait' (appears between the How Benign prose and the HbS Allelle section in the source) was dropped as EdApp navigation scaffolding (expandable-section label with no body text). Confirm this is correct.
candidates.reason: Bare bold header with no prose, no image, no list — typical EdApp collapsible trigger; merging its implied context into the HbS Allelle slide maintains content continuity.

candidates.suggestion: Clinical Manifestations sub-sections Papillary Necrosis, Splenic Infarction, Renal Medullary Carcinoma, and Hyphema and Glaucoma are bare headers with no body prose in the source. They are rendered as h3 headings inside the slide's rich_text to preserve their presence. If expanded content exists in a non-Markdown source, those sections should be fleshed out.
candidates.reason: Source has no prose under these headers — only the Splenic Infarction causation list has body text. Bare headers preserved verbatim inside a single consolidated slide rather than creating empty-body slides.

candidates.suggestion: The bolded conclusion statement ('Although sickle cell trait has been associated with many complications, the average life expectancy...') was rendered as a callout block with slide title 'Key Finding'. The slide title was inferred since the source has no explicit section header for this statement. Confirm title is acceptable or provide a preferred label.
candidates.reason: The statement appears as a standalone bold line in the source with no enclosing section header — a callout block conveys the emphasis, and 'Key Finding' is the minimal label needed by the slide title field.
